title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblymember Dr. Joaquin Arambula (D-Fresno) has introduced AB 2240, a farmworker housing bill, to ensure that farmworkers and their families are not forced to leave

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2240 seeks to authorize the California Department of Housing and Community Development to keep its 24 migratory farmworker housing centers open year-round.",
    "The closures of housing centers force farmworker families to lose affordable housing and scramble to find places to live.",
    "Eliminating the requirement that farmworkers and their families must live outside a 50-mile radius of their centers for at least three months adversely impacts the continuity of education for their children.",
    "AB 2240 aims to reduce unnecessary stress and instability for farmworker families.",
    "Year-round housing is believed to benefit students from K-12 with consistent education.",
    "Current housing conditions make it difficult for high school students to catch up academically after returning from Mexico.",
    "Some students miss substantial amounts of school, impacting their ability to graduate on time."
  ]
}
```
